result:
If I could change the world in one way, I would eliminate the capacity for self-deception in every human mind — not the ability to be wrong, but the deep, reflexive habit of protecting comforting lies over inconvenient truths.  

No more denying the damage we cause, the pain we ignore, the systems we sustain because changing them would cost us something. No more quieting conscience with rationalization. No more "I didn't know" when we did.  

It wouldn't make us perfect. It would make us honest. And from that honesty, everything else might follow: accountability, real compassion, the slow and painful work of actually fixing things instead of just believing we already have.
